methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $24M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Emerald AI is a seed-stage company in the Climate & Energy sector founded by Varun Sivaram, having raised $24.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private comparables (Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M), supplemented by a broader reference set of 8 private companies and 1 public comparable in frontier climate and energy technologies.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $24.5M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying a $61M valuation. The peer-median method anchors to the three same-sector comparables, which exhibit a median valuation of $150M with a range of $110M–$200M. This method's higher output reflects the relatively mature valuations of the selected peers relative to Emerald's current seed stage.

Triangulating these two methods yields a range of $52M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $106M. The wide spread reflects genuine uncertainty about appropriate stage discount or premium relative to the Series A peer set. Key drivers of valuation within this range include capital efficiency relative to peers, market timing for climate-tech, and de-risking progress toward Series A. The analysis is most sensitive to the stage adjustment assumption and the relevance of Series A comparables to a seed-stage target.
